title: Ensure that the admission control plugin PodSecurityPolicy is set

input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control plugin PodSecurityPolicy is set
input_row.description: Reject creating pods that do not match Pod Security Policies.
input_row.rationale: A Pod Security Policy is a cluster-level resource that controls the actions that a pod can perform and what it has the ability to access. The PodSecurityPolicy objects define a set of conditions that a pod must run with in order to be accepted into the system. Pod Security Policies are comprised of settings and strategies that control the security features a pod has access to and hence this must be used to control pod access permissions. Note: When the PodSecurityPolicy admission plugin is in use, there needs to be at least one PodSecurityPolicy in place for ANY pods to be admitted. See section 5.2 for recommendations on PodSecurityPolicy settings.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the master node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --enable-admission-plugins argument is set to a value that includes PodSecurityPolicy .
input_row.remediation: Follow the documentation and create Pod Security Policy objects as per your environment. Then, edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml on the master node and set the -- enable-admission-plugins parameter to a value that includes PodSecurityPolicy : --enable-admission-plugins=...,PodSecurityPolicy,... Then restart the API Server.
input_row.impact: The policy objects must be created and granted before pod creation would be allowed.
input_row.default_value: By default, PodSecurityPolicy is not set.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions using the command `ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver` to check the API server flags, which indicates that this can be automated.
- The requirement to check if the `--enable-admission-plugins` argument includes `PodSecurityPolicy` can be verified through the Kubernetes API or by inspecting the API server configuration.
- Since this is a standard Kubernetes service property and can be queried via Kubernetes APIs, the approach is Automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_admission_plugins_required_set

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the configuration of the API server.
- Specifically, you can retrieve the API server's configuration by checking the pod specifications for the kube-apiserver.
- The command to use is:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].args}'
  ```
- This command retrieves the arguments passed to the kube-apiserver container.
- **Pass/Fail criteria:**
  - Pass: The output contains `--enable-admission-plugins=...,PodSecurityPolicy,...`
  - Fail: The output does not contain `PodSecurityPolicy` in the `--enable-admission-plugins` argument.
- This ensures that the PodSecurityPolicy admission control plugin is set as required by the control.
